children.name: Good protein choice and plenty of vegetables. This
children.characters: You’ve already got a solid base with lean protein from the chicken salad, which is helpful going into a lift. The sushi also gives you some lighter carbs, which fits well with a low appetite before training. That combination can help provide steady energy without making you feel too full or sluggish during your workout. It’s a nice balance that supports both performance and comfort, especially if you’re not in the mood for a heavy pre-lift meal. Overall, it sets you up well to stay fueled while still feeling light and ready to move.
